title: sedum rosea var. neomexicanum
intro: Sedum rosea var. neomexicanum, also known as New Mexican stonecrop, is a succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant contains compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, offering potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Sedum rosea var. neomexicanum is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or cream to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
